data.title: Copper prices reach $12,000 for first time amid tariff disruptions
data.description:
Copper prices surged to a record above $12,000 a metric ton, propelled by a combination of supply disruptions at key mines and sharp distortions in global trade flows tied to U.S. tariff policy under President Donald Trump. The rally puts the metal on track for its strongest annual performance since the aftermath of the global financial crisis in 2009.

On the London Metal Exchange, copper climbed as much as 1% to an intraday peak of $12,044 a ton, extending a run that has pushed prices more than 30% higher this year. Concerns that the U.S. could impose tariffs on copper have played a pivotal role, prompting a wave of imports into the country and leaving buyers elsewhere scrambling to secure material.

The resulting shift in trade patterns has been so pronounced that prices have continued to rise despite weakening demand fundamentals in China, which accounts for roughly half of global copper consumption.

Although the metal is often seen as a gauge of worldwide industrial health, China’s slowdown has failed to halt the rally. Many traders now expect prices to continue climbing as shipments into the U.S. accelerate in anticipation of possible tariffs, Bloomberg News reported.

Supply-side pressures have added further momentum. Production interruptions at mines across the Americas, Africa and Asia have heightened concerns that the market is approaching a significant shortfall. Deutsche Bank has warned that output from the world’s largest copper producers is likely to decline about 3% this year, with the risk of further reductions in 2026.

Inventories remain adequate for the moment, but analysts at Morgan Stanley argue that the balance will deteriorate sharply next year. The bank forecasts that global demand could exceed supply by roughly 600,000 tons in 2026, marking the most severe deficit in more than two decades, with gaps expected to widen thereafter.

Longstanding concerns about supply constraints have been a recurring theme in bullish outlooks for copper, alongside expectations of rising demand from electric vehicles, renewable energy infrastructure and artificial intelligence-related applications. Citigroup has told clients that prices could climb as high as $15,000 a ton in an optimistic scenario, particularly if a softer U.S. dollar and lower interest rates increase copper’s appeal to investors.

Still, the surge has its skeptics. During the pandemic-era rally, copper prices ultimately stalled below $11,000 a ton as resistance from Chinese buyers intensified. This time, too, some analysts are urging caution. Goldman Sachs has noted that the recent gains have been driven largely by investor positioning around future tightness rather than immediate supply-and-demand conditions.

Even so, the bank continues to favor copper among industrial metals and recently raised its price projection for next year to $11,400 a ton.

As of late morning in London, copper was trading around $12,042.50 a ton on the LME. Other base metals also advanced, with nickel posting gains of roughly 2%.

data.title: Six Flags plans to open its first theme park outside of North America next week
data.description:
Six Flags Entertainment Corporation (FUN) is scheduled to open and operate its first theme park outside the U.S. next week in a notable development for the travel and leisure sector.

Six Flags Qiddiya City in Saudi Arabia will open on December 31 as the flagship theme park of Saudi Arabia's Qiddiya City giga-project. The opening will position the theme park at the center of the Qiddiya City project, which is seen as both a tourism catalyst and a symbolic milestone for Saudi Vision 2030’s push into large-scale entertainment and leisure. 

Six Flags (FUN) does not own Six Flags Qiddiya City, as the project is owned and funded by Qiddiya Investment Company, which is backed by Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund. For its part, Six Flags (FUN) is engaged under a management/services and licensing agreement to operate the park and provide its brand and expertise.

The theme park plans to welcome guests with a New Year's Eve launch that effectively rings in 2026 for Saudi Arabia’s emerging entertainment hub near Riyadh, located in the Tuwaiq Mountains about 40 minutes from the capital as part of a broader mixed-use destination combining entertainment, sports, culture, and residential development. At opening, Six Flags Qiddiya City will feature 28 rides and attractions across more than 320,000 square meters, including 10 major thrill rides and various family-focused experiences, with headline draws such as Falcons Flight, billed as the world’s tallest, fastest, and longest coaster, alongside Iron Rattler, promoted as the tallest tilt coaster, and Spitfire, described as the tallest inverted coaster, cementing the park’s ambition to be a global thrill destination. The attractions are organized into themed lands such as Steam Town, Twilight Gardens, Discovery Springs, City Thrills, Valley of Fortune, and Grand Exposition/City of Thrills, blending high-intensity rides with immersive environments.

The opening is significant because it marks Six Flags' (FUN) first from-the-ground-up international flagship developed in partnership with a sovereign-backed entity. Analysts have noted that the opening marks a strategic pivot by Six Flags (FUN) toward management and brand-licensing models in high-growth markets. Theme park industry observers call Six Flags Qiddiya City one of the most anticipated global theme park openings of the decade.

Shares of Six Flags (FUN) are down more than 66% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Hyperscale Data’s bitcoin holdings reach 514.97 BTC, exceeding 100% of market capitalization
data.description:
• Hyperscale Data (GPUS) Tuesday said that its Bitcoin treasury, including current holdings and cash allocated to committed purchases of Bitcoin, totaled approximately $76 million, based on the price of Bitcoin as of December 21.
  • This amount represents ~100.75% of the company's market capitalization, based on the company's stock price at the close of trading on December 22, 2025. 
  • Hyperscale Data's (GPUS) next goal is to reach $100 million in Bitcoin on its balance sheet.

data.title: Delta plans to become the first U.S. carrier with a direct flight to Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL) plans to launch nonstop service between Atlanta and Riyadh on October 23, 2026, using an Airbus A350-900. The new route will make Delta (DAL) the only U.S. carrier offering direct flights to Saudi Arabia and mark a major step in its strategy to tap fast-growing Middle East demand. 

The Riyadh flight will operate initially daily around launch before transitioning to three times weekly from early November, with block times of roughly 13 hours to Riyadh and about 15.5 hours back to Atlanta, which will make it among Delta's (DAL) longest routes. Atlanta’s role as Delta’s largest hub allows the carrier to funnel extensive domestic and Latin American feed into the service, while Riyadh’s King Khalid International Airport is being developed as a global hub under Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030, which aims to diversify the economy through tourism, aviation, and investment. The route is reported to be supported by significant government subsidies aimed at positioning Riyadh as a premier global aviation hub.

Strategically, the launch is seen as significant inside the airline industry because it anchors Delta's (DAL) new exclusive partnership with start-up carrier Riyadh Air, under which Delta will be Riyadh Air’s sole North American partner and Riyadh Air will be Delta’s exclusive partner in Riyadh. The two carrier plan for codesharing, reciprocal connectivity, loyalty cooperation, and broader operational collaboration in areas such as maintenance and ground handling. The partnership creates an important hub-to-hub corridor between North America and an emerging Middle East hub, giving Delta (DAL) a proprietary access point into the region rather than relying on passengers self-connecting via Gulf carriers like Emirates or Qatar Airways.

data.title: Jack in the Box to focus on its core brand after closing on sale of the Del Taco chain
data.description:
Jack in the Box (JACK) announced that it completed the sale of Del Taco Holdings to Yadav Enterprises Inc. for $119 million. The company said the transaction is an important step in its "Jack on Track" plan to strengthen the balance sheet and accelerate its shift toward a simpler, asset-light business model.

In connection with the closing, Jack in the Box (JACK) received approximately $109 million of the consideration in cash and the remaining $10 million in the form of a 21-day promissory note, accruing interest at an 8% annual rate.

"Our sale of Del Taco represents meaningful progress in simplifying our business model and reducing our debt. We remain committed to elevating the Jack in the Box brand and improving operational performance to drive sustainable, long-term growth and create value for our shareholders," highlighted CEO Lance Tucker. 

Jack in the Box (JACK) agreed to acquire Del Taco back in December of 2021 for all-cash consideration of $12.51 per share, implying a deal value of about $575 million to $585 million. The transaction closed on March 7, 2022, at which point Del Taco became a wholly owned subsidiary of Jack in the Box (JACK). At the time, Jack in the Box (JACK) highlighted that combining its brand with Del Taco's Mexican QSR platform would create a larger QSR player with enhanced scale, overlapping guest profiles, and complementary menus and cultures. However, Jack in the Box (JACK) decided to refocus on the core Jack in the Box brand after several weak quarters and underperformance at both concepts.

Shares of Jack in the Box (JACK) are down more than 53% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Trump administration considers giving Musk’s SpaceX 800 acres in Texas wildlife refuge: report
data.description:
• The Trump administration is considering giving nearly 800 acres of land in a federal wildlife refuge in Texas to SpaceX (SPACE), the rocket and satellite maker run by Elon Musk, according to documents reviewed by The New York Times.
  • The company would use the land to expand its rocket launch and production site in Cameron County, Texas.
  • In exchange, SpaceX (SPACE) would give the government hundreds of acres of its own property, some of which is about 20 miles from the refuge, the documents show.
  • The proposed exchange, which has not previously been reported, has alarmed some conservationists and archaeologists, the report said. They worry that SpaceX could degrade tracts that are home to numerous endangered species as well as artifacts from a Civil War-era battlefield.

data.title: Sanofi, Regeneron’s Dupixent approved in Japan for children with asthma
data.description:
• Sanofi (SNY) and Regeneron’s (REGN) asthma treatment, Dupixent, has been approved in Japan for children aged 6 to 11, marking a significant expansion of the drug’s pediatric use.
  • This expands the previous approval in Japan in this indication for patients aged 12 years and older.
  • Dupixent is the first and only biologic medicine to demonstrate improved lung function in this young patient group in a randomized phase 3 study, and inhibits IL-4 and IL-13, two key and central drivers of type 2 inflammation, the companies said.
  • In addition to asthma, Dupixent is approved in Japan in certain patients with atopic dermatitis, chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyposis (CRSwNP), prurigo nodularis, chronic spontaneous urticaria, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

data.title: Trump renews push for Greenland takeover, names envoy to 'lead the charge'
data.description:
President Donald Trump has once again said that the U.S. needs to take over Greenland for its national security, and appointed Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry as his special envoy to the island to "lead the charge."

"We need Greenland for national security, not for minerals," Trump told reporters at his Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida on Monday. "If you take a look at Greenland, you look up and down the coast, you have Russian and Chinese ships all over the place… We have to have it."

"It's an honor to serve you in this volunteer position to make Greenland a part of the U.S. This in no way affects my position as Governor of Louisiana," Landry posted on X.

Greenland is a semi-autonomous territory of Denmark that has substantial mineral deposits, which could potentially help the U.S. reduce its reliance on Chinese exports.

The prime ministers of Greenland and Denmark both criticized Trump's remarks. "Greenland belongs to Greenlanders," Denmark's Mette Frederiksen and Greenland's Jens-Frederik Nielsen said in a joint statement.

"You cannot annex another country. Not even with an argument about international security," they added. "We expect respect for our joint territorial integrity."

Danish Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen told local media that he would summon the U.S. ambassador to Denmark to discuss Trump's appointment of a special envoy to Greenland.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ZIM, NVO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures nudged higher in a quiet Tuesday morning trade, signaling steady nerves among investors navigating a holiday-shortened week with unusually low volumes before markets wrap early Wednesday and pause fully for Christmas.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • ZIM Integrated Shipping Services (ZIM) +12% - Shares surged after the company said it has received proposals from multiple strategic parties as part of its ongoing review of strategic alternatives. ZIM also disclosed receiving a revised bid from an entity owned by CEO Eli Glickman and shipping magnate Rami Ungar, which the board rejected after concluding it significantly undervalued the company. The strategic review, launched last month following an initial proposal from Glickman and Ungar, is now in an advanced stage and includes a potential sale of the company, capital allocation initiatives, and possible shareholder returns. The update follows earlier media reports that Hapag-Lloyd has also submitted an offer for ZIM.
  • Novo Nordisk (NVO) +9% - Shares climbed after the U.S. FDA approved an oral version of the company’s blockbuster weight-loss drug Wegovy (semaglutide), marking the first GLP-1 pill approved for obesity. The decision was based on results from the Phase 3 OASIS 4 trial, which showed that daily oral semaglutide 25 mg delivered a mean weight loss of 16.6% in adults who were obese or overweight with at least one comorbidity. Novo Nordisk said the Wegovy pill is expected to launch in January 2026.
  • Parsons (PSN) +6% - Shares rose after the company won the Missile Defense Agency’s Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD) IDIQ contract, which carries a ceiling value of up to $151B. The contract spans a broad range of work areas aimed at accelerating the development and delivery of innovative defense capabilities to the warfighter.

data.title: Wing Yip Food gets Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Wing Yip Food (WYHG) received a written notice from the Nasdaq Stock Market on December 22, 2025, notifying the company that it is not in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company has been provided 180 calendar days, or until June 22, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The stock price traded ~1.7% higher on Monday during after-market hours.
